resources.string:
This app has no UI of its own. It registers two independent Locus sensor adapters, one per device type, so each example stays focused:

• "Heart rate monitor" (BT4 / GATT) — any standard BLE Heart Rate strap (Polar, Wahoo, Garmin, Suunto, …) → heart rate.
• "NMEA GNSS speed" (BT3 / SPP) — a Bluetooth-Classic NMEA GNSS receiver → speed, parsed from RMC sentences.

To try one:
1. Install Locus Map on this device.
2. For the BT3 adapter, first pair the receiver in Android Bluetooth settings.
3. In Locus, open the sensor picker and add a sensor.
4. Under "External adapters", pick the device type. Locus drives the connection; these services only decode the bytes.

The parsers live in HrmAdapterService.kt and NmeaSpeedAdapterService.kt — each tiny. Copy either module as the starting point for your own adapter.

Locus shows a "Settings" action on the HRM adapter's picker row, wired here to this screen via getIntentForSettings(); the NMEA service omits that override, since it's optional.
